name: Brantwood
description_short: Brantwood Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving Wisconsin since 1932, offering checking, savings, and loan services with flexible payment options.
description_long: Brantwood Credit Union was established in 1932 and operates as a member-owned financial institution based in Brantwood, Wisconsin. The credit union has maintained continuous service to its membership for over 90 years, positioning itself as a community-focused alternative to traditional banks. The organization maintains a physical presence with specific hours of operation and accessible contact methods including phone, fax, and email. Brantwood Credit Union offers core banking services typical of credit unions, including member accounts and loan products. They provide multiple payment options for loan servicing, recognizing the need for convenience in how members manage their finances. The credit union has invested in modernizing payment infrastructure by accepting both online debit card payments and electronic checks (eChecks) through ACH processing. Additionally, they maintain a partnership with American Income Life Insurance Company to provide accidental death and dismemberment (AD&D) benefits and other insurance products to members at no cost. What distinguishes Brantwood is its limited operational hours (closed Wednesday and Thursday except by appointment, closed weekends) and its relatively small-town presence in Wisconsin. The organization has adapted to modern consumer expectations by offering digital payment methods while maintaining traditional banking hours and in-person service. Their partnership approach to insurance and benefits suggests a collaborative model rather than comprehensive in-house offerings. Brantwood Credit Union represents a traditional community credit union model. While the website confirms their existence and basic service offerings, the limited information available suggests a smaller-scale operation compared to regional or national credit unions. The restricted hours and appointment-only days may indicate capacity constraints or intentional operational choices. Members should expect personalized service and community focus but should verify specific loan products, interest rates, and account features directly, as these details are not disclosed on the website.
services: Debit card payment processing for loans ($7 fee)
eCheck/ACH electronic payments for loan payments ($2 fee)
In-person banking services during specified hours
Accidental Death & Dismemberment insurance benefits (through partnership)
Insurance products through American Income Life Insurance Company
pros: Established credit union with 90+ years of operating history since 1932, indicating stability and member trust
Multiple payment options including online debit card payments and eChecks via ACH network
No-cost AD&D and insurance benefits through American Income Life Insurance partnership
Local, community-focused institution providing personalized service in Brantwood, Wisconsin
Flexible payment methods recognizing members with different preferences (online, eCheck, in-person)
Multiple contact channels available (phone, fax, email, mailing address, in-person)
Transparent fee structure disclosed for online and eCheck payments ($7 and $2 respectively)
cons: Very limited operational hours with Wednesday and Thursday available only by appointment, and no weekend service
Minimal information on website about loan products, interest rates, account types, and specific services offered
High online payment processing fee of $7.00 per transaction may be discouraging for frequent bill payers
eCheck processing takes 5-7 business days, requiring advance planning for month-end payments
Limited geographic accessibility for non-local members with only one physical location in small Wisconsin town
best_for: Local Brantwood and surrounding Wisconsin residents seeking community-focused banking with personal relationships
Credit union members preferring traditional, established institutions with long operating histories
Consumers comfortable with limited operational hours in exchange for local, personalized service
diagnosis: Brantwood Credit Union is best for local Wisconsin residents or existing members seeking traditional credit union banking with community service and a long institutional history. The main caveat is severely limited operational hours (closed Wednesdays/Thursdays except by appointment, no weekend service) and sparse online information about specific products, requiring direct contact to learn about loan types, rates, and account features.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Brantwood — Credit Union in Brantwood, WI | CreditDoc
meta_description: Brantwood — NCUA-insured credit union in Brantwood, WI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
